selected_items.title: Cancer cells can use fatty acid oxidation, not just glucose fermentation
selected_items.novelty: Directly challenges the Warburg/Seyfried glucose-fermentation-only model by stating that cancer cells with defective mitochondria can still generate energy from fatty acids, contradicting the 'cut glucose, eat saturated fat, kill cancer' protocol
selected_items.mechanism: Cancer cells are not limited to glucose and glutamine fermentation; some tumors retain or adapt fatty acid oxidation pathways despite mitochondrial dysfunction
selected_items.evidence: Kruse calls the quoted claim 'pure nonsense' and labels the source a 'trader scammer,' implying the Warburg-only model is incomplete or wrong
selected_items.practical_value: Warns against oversimplified ketogenic cancer protocols that assume all cancers are glucose-dependent; some cancers may thrive on fat metabolism
selected_items.writer_brief: Explain why the 'cancer only ferments glucose' model is incomplete, what it means for tumors to use fatty acid oxidation, and why this changes the risk profile of high-fat cancer protocols. Cite the Warburg/Seyfried framing and the rebuttal.
selected_items.risk_notes: Do not claim all cancers use fat or all cancers ferment glucose. The point is heterogeneity and the danger of one-size-fits-all metabolic cancer protocols.

selected_items.title: Triplet oxygen and Type 1 collagen in skull deformation
selected_items.novelty: Links lack of triplet oxygen to Type 1 collagen defects, explaining skull deformations (sagittal suture absence, cranial capacity changes) through oxygen chemistry rather than head-binding
selected_items.mechanism: Triplet oxygen (ground-state molecular oxygen) is required for proper Type 1 collagen synthesis and cross-linking; deficiency leads to structural collagen defects visible in skull geometry
selected_items.evidence: Reference to recent podcast (linked); response to skull deformation observation
selected_items.practical_value: Reframes ancient skull anomalies as oxygen/collagen biology rather than cultural practice; connects to modern collagen health and oxygen availability
selected_items.writer_brief: Explain what triplet oxygen is, how it affects Type 1 collagen, and why this matters for skull structure. Connect to the podcast and the broader collagen/oxygen thesis.
selected_items.risk_notes: Do not overclaim about ancient skulls or head-binding. The source gives a mechanism but not a full case study.

selected_items.title: New podcast on biophysics of modern patients
selected_items.novelty: Kruse describes this as his most enjoyable 2026 podcast, focused on high-level decentralized medicine between two clinicians
selected_items.mechanism: Biophysics of modern patients in the context of decentralized medicine; clinical application of light, magnetism, water, and redox principles
selected_items.evidence: Podcast link provided; Kruse endorsement as 'high level decentralized medicine at its finest'
selected_items.practical_value: Likely contains clinical case discussion, protocol refinement, and mechanism clarification; high signal for practitioners and advanced members
selected_items.writer_brief: Announce the podcast, note Kruse's endorsement, and flag it as a high-value clinical resource. Do not summarize content unless you have access to the full podcast.
selected_items.risk_notes: Do not invent podcast content. Only note that it exists and is endorsed.

selected_items.title: PMLE and doxycycline as deuterium flash-evaporation accelerator
selected_items.novelty: Reframes Polymorphous Light Eruption (PMLE) and cutaneous lupus as a deuterium flash-evaporation event, not a sun allergy; explains doxycycline photosensitivity as a molecular antenna that accelerates the evaporation event and forces activation of the POMC-melanin shield
selected_items.mechanism: Winter low-light/low-grounding allows deuterium to accumulate in skin extracellular fluid; vagus and ventricular drainage systems are lattice-locked by heavy isotope load; skin becomes backup exhaust manifold; doxycycline's four-ring hydronaphthacene nucleus intercalates into deuterium-sludgified skin lattice, hyper-accelerating flash-evaporation and forcing a choice between systemic Matrix Fire or Chromosome 2 POMC-melanin spintronic shield activation
selected_items.evidence: Kruse forum post with detailed mechanism; references MITF-AMPAR, vagus drain pipe, 70-degree ventricular Venturi pump, and doxycycline molecular structure
selected_items.practical_value: Explains why PMLE flares in spring, why doxycycline triggers photosensitivity, and why the reaction is not an allergy but a biophysical evaporation event; suggests grounding and deuterium management as interventions
selected_items.writer_brief: Explain PMLE as a deuterium flash-evaporation event, how doxycycline acts as a light-harvesting antenna, and why this reframes 'sun allergy' as a drainage/isotope problem. Translate the mechanism into plain language.
selected_items.risk_notes: Do not claim this replaces medical diagnosis or treatment. This is a mechanistic reframe, not a protocol override.

selected_items.title: Acute lattice lock subtracts consciousness
selected_items.novelty: Claims that acute lattice lock (sudden heavy deuterium or electromagnetic load) subtracts consciousness
selected_items.mechanism: Lattice lock (deuterium or nnEMF-induced rigidity in water lattice) disrupts mitochondrial redox and neural signaling, reducing consciousness
selected_items.evidence: Kruse forum post with link to tweet thread
selected_items.practical_value: Explains acute cognitive decline, brain fog, or loss of awareness as a lattice lock event; suggests rapid environmental change (nnEMF, deuterium, grounding loss) as a trigger
selected_items.writer_brief: Explain what lattice lock is, how it can happen acutely, and why it subtracts consciousness. Connect to practical triggers like nnEMF exposure, deuterium load, or grounding loss.
selected_items.risk_notes: Do not claim this explains all cognitive decline or neurological events. This is a biophysical mechanism, not a diagnosis.
